---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
  \usepackage{tikz}
  \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta, positioning, shapes.geometric}
  \usetikzlibrary{overlay-beamer-styles}
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commmented) -->

```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{6.3 - Assessing Reliability}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```

## Some considerations

Reliability is assessed in many cases by two main analyses: Pearson Correlation and ICC (keeping aside Internal Consistency).
\pause

These slides contain some aspects you should pay attention to.

## Test-Retest and Pearson correlation

If Pearson correlation is used, this must be complemented by a check that NO systematic effect is present (this is typically done with a t-test comparing the scores at the two timepoints), accompanied by Bland-Altman Plots.
```{=latex}
for details see \href{https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4470095/}{\underline{here}}, (Giavarina, 2015)
```


## Test-Retest and ICC
There are many ICC coefficients, the one most commonly used is **ICC(2,1) agreement**.

```{=latex}
\vfill
See \href{https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4913118/pdf/main.pdf}{\underline{here}} for a detailed overview (Koo and Li, 2016).
```

## Test-Retest, inter-rater and ICC

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{figure}
\includegraphics[scale=0.3]{Figures/wiki_ICC.png}
\end{figure}
\small
\href{https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intraclass_correlation}{\underline{ICC (Wikipedia)}}
\end{center}
```
